description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the docker:latest image with a docker:dind service, caching the node_modules directory and running a before_script that updates apk, installs jq and yarn, and runs yarn install for all jobs. Define three stages—test, publish, and release. In the test stage add a test job that executes `yarn run test`. In the publish stage add a publish:npm job that runs only on tags matching the pattern `/v\d*\.\d*\.\d*/` (excluding branch pipelines), runs `yarn run make`, writes an .npmrc containing the NPM_TOKEN, and publishes the package with `npm publish --access=public`. In the release stage add a release:docker.io job that also runs only on version tags, runs `yarn run make`, extracts the package version via `export PACKAGE_VERSION=$(cat package.json | jq -r .version)`, logs into a Docker registry using `$DOCKER_REGISTRY_USER` and `$DOCKER_REGISTRY_PASSWORD`, builds the image with `docker build --pull -t "$DOCKER_REGISTRY_IMAGE" .`, and pushes it with `docker push "$DOCKER_REGISTRY_IMAGE"`. Also in the release stage add a release:gitlab job with the same tag triggers that runs `yarn run make`, extracts the version, logs into the GitLab Container Registry using `$CI_REGISTRY_USER` and `$CI_REGISTRY_PASSWORD`, builds the image with `docker build --pull -t "$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE" .`, and pushes it with `docker push "$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE"`.
